---
title: "Equivalence with tna: imbalanced actors"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>",
  message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10, fig.height = 7, dpi = 150
)
source("_equivalence-helpers.R")
```

20 sessions (length 10--18) with an extremely imbalanced actor split:
the Human partition has only one code (`h_only`), while AI has five
(`a1`-`a5`). The single-Human row of the transition matrix is
degenerate -- there is only one possible source state for any
Human-originating edge. This stresses how each engine handles
singleton states and unbalanced node groups.

The pipeline below runs the same htna ↔ tna ↔ Nestimate three-way
equivalence checks as the
[main equivalence article](tna-equivalence.html).

## Setup

```{r}
library(tna)
library(htna)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

ds <- ds_imbalanced()
```

```{r child = "_equivalence-body.Rmd"}
```
